title: Recipe for soy-sauce eggs
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"55aft","text":"This protocol describes a simple recipe for preparing soy-sauce–marinated eggs. The procedure is designed to produce a consistent culinary outcome and to illustrate core principles of experimental protocols (e.g., documentation, structure, and reproducibility). It draws inspiration from Crying in H Mart: A Memoir by Michelle Zauner, the 2026 UCSB Reads selection. Of note, soy-sauce eggs exist in many variations across cultures and kitchens, and this protocol represents only one such version.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":208,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":288,"length":26},{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":335,"length":29}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":335,"length":29}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"882E916AEEAA11F0A6E80A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://guides.library.ucsb.edu/ucsbreads2026"}}}}

steps: Bring water to a boil : fill a saucepan with enough water to fully cover the eggs and bring it to a rolling boil over high heat

Add eggs gently : use a slotted spoon or tongs to gently lower the eggs into the boiling water (to prevent them from cracking). Once all eggs are in, reduce the heat slightly to maintain a gentle boil

Set a timer : immediately start a timer for your desired level of doneness 00:06:00 : runny yolks, barely set whites 00:08:00 : jammy yolks, soft-set whites [Recommended] 00:10:00 : fully cooked yolks, firm whites
Stop the cooking : when the timer goes off, use a slotted spoon to quickly remove the eggs and plunge them into a large bowl of cold water

Cool and peel : Let the eggs rest in the cold water bath for at least 00:10:00 before peeling ‱ Use clean hands and utensils when peeling and transferring eggs For easy peeling, tap the wider bottom end (which often has a small air pocket) on a hard surface and peel under cold running water to help loosen the shell.

Combine ingredients for the marinade : while the eggs are cooling, add the following ingredients in a mixing bowl and stir until well combined Soy sauce: 120 mL (or 0.5 cup ) Water: 70 mL (or 0.3 cup ) Honey: 30 mL (or 2 tbsp ) Sesame oil: 15 mL (or 1 tbsp ) Rice vinegar: 15 mL (or 1 tbsp ) Smash garlic: 5 cloves [Optional] Dry chili flakes: 1 pinch [Optional] green onion, finely chopped: 1 stalk
Submerge the eggs : place peeled eggs in a sealable container and pour the marinade over the eggs Image of eggs submerged in marinade. Photo by Xiuqi Li.

Seal and refrigerate for 12:00:00 to Overnight ‱ Keep eggs at 4 °C or below during marination and storage ‱ Consume within 5 days Egg whites will be light-brown and savory; yolks creamy and slightly jammy. Flavor increases with longer marination.
Proceed to serve the eggs after marination
Slice a soy-sauce egg in half
Place over hot rice in a bowl [Optional] Top rice with some seaweed
Spoon 1-2 tbsp of marinade over the rice
Arrange noodles in a bowl and top with eggs
Slice a soy-sauce egg in half
Add 1-2 tbsp of marinade or sesame oil
creator: Jade Li
